long_tail_signals.Sentence: Cognitive and emotional behavioral impairments in LDL receptor knockout (LDLr-/-) mice are associated with neuroinflammation, blood-brain barrier dysfunction, impaired neurogenesis, brain oxidative stress, and mitochondrial dysfunction.

long_tail_signals.Hypothesis: Neuroinflammation Hypothesis,Oxidative Stress Hypothesis,Mitochondrial Autophagy Hypothesis
long_tail_signals.Event: ('TOMM40', 'Gene', 'dbSNP', ('TOMM40', 'Gene', '10452', '-')) -- ThemeOf -- ('F113L', 'Var', '(66, 71)', ('F113L', 'Mutation', 'rs157581', '-')) -- CauseOf -- ('induced', 'Reg', '(91, 98)', ('induced', 'Reg', '-', '-')) -- ThemeOf -- ('mitochondrial dysfunction', 'CPA', '(99, 124)', ('mitochondrial dysfunction', 'CPA', 'HP:0001427', 'Mitochondrial inheritance'))
long_tail_signals.EventDedupKey: term | tomm40 | normalized variants:dna change | neuroinflammation hypothesis,oxidative stress hypothesis,mitochondrial autophagy hypothesis
long_tail_signals.Sentence: When expressed in BV2 microglial cells, the AD-associated mutant (F113L) or (F131L) TOMM40 induced mitochondrial dysfunction and oxidative stress-induced activation of microglia and NLRP3 inflammasome.

long_tail_signals.Event: ('TOMM40', 'Gene', '(55, 61)', ('TOMM40', 'Gene', '10452', '-')) -- ThemeOf -- ('variation', 'Var', '(62, 71)', ('variation', 'Mutation', 'point mutations:Mutations', 'point mutations:Mutations')) -- CauseOf -- ('role', 'Reg', '(47, 51)', ('role', 'Reg', '-', '-')) -- ThemeOf -- ('mitochondrial dysfunction', 'Disease', '(75, 100)', ('mitochondrial dysfunction', 'Disease', 'MESH:D028361', '-'))
long_tail_signals.EventDedupKey: term | tomm40 | point mutations:mutations | neuroinflammation hypothesis,mitochondrial autophagy hypothesis
long_tail_signals.Sentence: We further utilized cell models to examine the role of TOMM40 variation in mitochondrial dysfunction that causes microglial activation and neuroinflammation.

long_tail_signals.Event: ('FRMD6', 'Gene', '(40, 45)', ('FRMD6', 'Gene', '319710', '-')) -- ThemeOf -- ('knockdown', 'Var', '(46, 55)', ('knockdown', 'Mutation', 'genetic manipulation:knockout', 'genetic manipulation:knockout')) -- CauseOf -- ('leads', 'Reg', '(56, 61)', ('leads', 'Reg', '-', '-')) -- ThemeOf -- ('mitochondrial dysfunction', 'Disease', '(65, 90)', ('mitochondrial dysfunction', 'Disease', 'MESH:D028361', '-'))
long_tail_signals.EventDedupKey: term | frmd6 | genetic manipulation:knockout | oxidative stress hypothesis,mitochondrial autophagy hypothesis
long_tail_signals.Sentence: Furthermore, we demonstrate that Willin/FRMD6 knockdown leads to mitochondrial dysfunction and fragmentation, as well as upregulation of ERK1/2 signaling, both of which are reported to be key early features of AD pathogenesis.

long_tail_signals.Event: ('NEIL1', 'Gene', '(35, 40)', ('NEIL1', 'Gene', '72774', '-')) -- ThemeOf -- ('Knockout', 'Var', '(0, 8)', ('Knockout', 'Mutation', 'genetic manipulation:knockout', 'genetic manipulation:knockout')) -- CauseOf -- ('results in', 'Reg', '(119, 129)', ('results in', 'Reg', '-', '-')) -- ThemeOf -- ('mitochondrial dysfunction', 'CPA', '(130, 155)', ('mitochondrial dysfunction', 'CPA', 'HP:0001427', 'Mitochondrial inheritance'))
long_tail_signals.EventDedupKey: term | neil1 | genetic manipulation:knockout | oxidative stress hypothesis,mitochondrial autophagy hypothesis
long_tail_signals.Sentence: Knockout of mitochondrial OGG1 and NEIL1, which remove 8-oxo-dG and formamidopyrimidine lesions, respectively, in mice results in mitochondrial dysfunction and a metabolic syndrome phenotype.

long_tail_signals.Event: ('OGG1', 'Gene', '(26, 30)', ('OGG1', 'Gene', '18294', '-')) -- ThemeOf -- ('Knockout', 'Var', '(0, 8)', ('Knockout', 'Mutation', 'genetic manipulation:knockout', 'genetic manipulation:knockout')) -- CauseOf -- ('results in', 'Reg', '(119, 129)', ('results in', 'Reg', '-', '-')) -- ThemeOf -- ('mitochondrial dysfunction', 'CPA', '(130, 155)', ('mitochondrial dysfunction', 'CPA', 'HP:0001427', 'Mitochondrial inheritance'))
long_tail_signals.EventDedupKey: term | ogg1 | genetic manipulation:knockout | oxidative stress hypothesis,mitochondrial autophagy hypothesis
long_tail_signals.Sentence: Knockout of mitochondrial OGG1 and NEIL1, which remove 8-oxo-dG and formamidopyrimidine lesions, respectively, in mice results in mitochondrial dysfunction and a metabolic syndrome phenotype.

long_tail_signals.Event: ('FXN', 'Gene', '(43, 46)', ('FXN', 'Gene', '2395', '-')) -- ThemeOf -- ('silencing', 'Var', '(30, 39)', ('silencing', 'Mutation', 'epigenic changes:silencing', 'epigenic changes:silencing')) -- CauseOf -- ('caused', 'Reg', '(52, 58)', ('caused', 'Reg', '-', '-')) -- ThemeOf -- ('mitochondrial dysfunction', 'CPA', '(59, 84)', ('mitochondrial dysfunction', 'CPA', 'HP:0003287', 'Abnormality of mitochondrial metabolism'))
long_tail_signals.EventDedupKey: term | fxn | epigenic changes:silencing | mitochondrial autophagy hypothesis
long_tail_signals.Sentence: The authors observed that the silencing of FXN gene caused mitochondrial dysfunction leading to cellular senescence and an increase in autophagy.

long_tail_signals.Event: ('apoE4', 'Gene', '(13, 18)', ('apoE4', 'Gene', '348', '-')) -- ThemeOf -- ('silencing', 'Var', '(284, 293)', ('silencing', 'Mutation', 'epigenic changes:silencing', 'epigenic changes:silencing')) -- CauseOf -- ('promoted', 'PosReg', '(45, 53)', ('promoted', 'PosReg', '-', '-')) -- ThemeOf -- ('mitochondrial dysfunction', 'CPA', '(72, 97)', ('mitochondrial dysfunction', 'CPA', 'HP:0001427', 'Mitochondrial inheritance'))
long_tail_signals.EventDedupKey: term | apoe | epigenic changes:silencing | oxidative stress hypothesis,mitochondrial autophagy hypothesis
long_tail_signals.Sentence: Furthermore, apoE4 (Delta272-299) expression promoted GRP75 expression, mitochondrial dysfunction and calcium transport into the mitochondria in neuron, which were significantly mitigated by treatment with PBA (an inhibitor of ER stress), MKT077 (a specific GRP75 inhibitor) or GRP75 silencing.

long_tail_signals.Event: ('WFS1', 'Gene', '(17, 21)', ('WFS1', 'Gene', '83725', '-')) -- ThemeOf -- ('Silencing', 'Var', '(0, 9)', ('Silencing', 'Mutation', 'epigenic changes:silencing', 'epigenic changes:silencing')) -- CauseOf -- ('upregulated', 'PosReg', '(75, 86)', ('upregulated', 'PosReg', '-', '-')) -- ThemeOf -- ('mitochondrial dysfunction and degeneration', 'Disease', '(104, 146)', ('mitochondrial dysfunction and degeneration', 'Disease', 'MESH:D028361', '-'))
long_tail_signals.EventDedupKey: term | wfs1 | epigenic changes:silencing | mitochondrial autophagy hypothesis
long_tail_signals.Sentence: Silencing of the WFS1 gene in HEK cells with small interfering RNA (siRNA) upregulated genes related to mitochondrial dysfunction and degeneration, suggesting that loss of wolframin precipitates degeneration via mitochondrial dysfunction.

long_tail_signals.Event: ('CISD2', 'Gene', '(0, 5)', ('CISD2', 'Gene', '493856', '-')) -- ThemeOf -- ('deficiency', 'Var', '(6, 16)', ('deficiency', 'Mutation', 'structural variation:deletion', 'structural variation:deletion')) -- CauseOf -- ('contributes', 'Reg', '(67, 78)', ('contributes', 'Reg', '-', '-')) -- ThemeOf -- ('mitochondrial dysfunctions', 'Disease', '(82, 108)', ('mitochondrial dysfunctions', 'Disease', 'MESH:D028361', '-'))
long_tail_signals.EventDedupKey: term | cisd2 | structural variation:deletion | oxidative stress hypothesis,mitochondrial autophagy hypothesis
long_tail_signals.Sentence: CISD2 deficiency enhances the expression of iNOS and RANTES, which contributes to mitochondrial dysfunctions, such as low DeltaPsi(m) levels, high ROS levels, and augmented apoptosis, which are mitigated upon curcumin treatment.

long_tail_signals.Event: ('BACE1', 'Gene', '(239, 244)', ('BACE1', 'Gene', '23821', '-')) -- ThemeOf -- ('inhibition', 'Var', '(18, 28)', ('inhibition', 'Mutation', 'expression changes:underexpression', 'expression changes:underexpression')) -- CauseOf -- ('restored', 'PosReg', '(48, 56)', ('restored', 'PosReg', '-', '-')) -- ThemeOf -- ('mitochondrial dysfunctions', 'Disease', '(87, 113)', ('mitochondrial dysfunctions', 'Disease', 'MESH:D028361', '-'))
long_tail_signals.EventDedupKey: term | bace1 | expression changes:underexpression | amyloid hypothesis,mitochondrial autophagy hypothesis
long_tail_signals.Sentence: In another study, inhibition of Drp1 by Mdivi-1 restored amyloid-beta (Abeta)-mediated mitochondrial dysfunctions and synaptic depression in neurons and significantly reduced lipid peroxidation, the expression of the Ab processing enzyme, BACE1, as well as Abeta deposition in the brain of AD mice.

long_tail_signals.Event: ('NRF1', 'Gene', '(42, 46)', ('NRF1', 'Gene', '18181', '-')) -- ThemeOf -- ('inactivation', 'Var', '(26, 38)', ('inactivation', 'Mutation', 'expression changes:inactivation', 'expression changes:inactivation')) -- CauseOf -- ('leads', 'Reg', '(101, 106)', ('leads', 'Reg', '-', '-')) -- ThemeOf -- ('mitochondrial dysfunction', 'Disease', '(110, 135)', ('mitochondrial dysfunction', 'Disease', 'MESH:D028361', '-'))
long_tail_signals.EventDedupKey: term | nrf1 | expression changes:inactivation | oxidative stress hypothesis,mitochondrial autophagy hypothesis
long_tail_signals.Sentence: Thus, either depletion or inactivation of NRF1 will lead to an impairment in OXPHOS which ultimately leads to mitochondrial dysfunction and oxidative stress inside cells.

long_tail_signals.Sentence: The common molecular mechanisms bridging the two disorders include chromosomal missegregation, overproduction of amyloid precursor protein, oxidative stress and mitochondrial dysfunction, nuclear factor of activated T cells (NFAT) and tau phosphorylation pathways, endocytic pathway abnormality, mutation in amyloid precursor protein gene (APP), presence of Apolipoprotein E epsilon 4 (APOE epsilon4) allele.

long_tail_signals.Sentence: In the present study, we addressed the role of Mfn2 in the hiPSCs differentiation system and report here that knockdown of Mfn2 results in mitochondrial dysfunctions and defects in neurogenesis and synapse formation.

long_tail_signals.Hypothesis: Amyloid Hypothesis,Oxidative Stress Hypothesis,Mitochondrial Autophagy Hypothesis

long_tail_signals.Sentence: Consistent with these changes in mitochondrial C99/fl-APP levels, BACE1(+/-) deletion age-dependently rescued mitochondrial dysfunction in 5XFAD mice, as assessed by cytochrome c release from mitochondria, reduced redox or complex activities and oxidative DNA damage.

long_tail_signals.Sentence: Moreover, recent reports have evidenced that PTN is implicated in the regulation of peripheral metainflammation, metabolic homeostasis, thermogenesis, as well as insulin sensitivity in the peripheral tissues Furthermore, Ptn deletion protects against neuroinflammation, mitochondrial dysfunction, and aberrant protein aggregation in a high fat diet (HFD) induced obesity model.

long_tail_signals.Event: ('catalase', 'Gene', '(180, 188)', ('catalase', 'Gene', '12359', '-')) -- ThemeOf -- ('hyperphosphorylated', 'Var', '(35, 54)', ('hyperphosphorylated', 'Mutation', 'epigenic changes:phosphorylation', 'epigenic changes:phosphorylation')) -- CauseOf -- ('leading to', 'Reg', '(299, 309)', ('leading to', 'Reg', '-', '-')) -- ThemeOf -- ('mitochondrial dysfunction', 'CPA', '(310, 335)', ('mitochondrial dysfunction', 'CPA', 'HP:0001427', 'Mitochondrial inheritance'))
long_tail_signals.EventDedupKey: term | cat | epigenic changes:phosphorylation | tau protein hypothesis,oxidative stress hypothesis
long_tail_signals.Sentence: Recent research also suggests that hyperphosphorylated Tau induces free radical production and lipid peroxidation and reduces antioxidant enzymes (e.g., glutathione peroxidase and catalase), causing an imbalance between the production of free radicals and endogenous antioxidant enzymes, ultimately leading to mitochondrial dysfunction in AD and other tauopathies.

long_tail_signals.Event: ('APOE4', 'Gene', '(0, 5)', ('APOE4', 'Gene', '348', '-')) -- ThemeOf -- ('expression', 'Var', '(6, 16)', ('expression', 'Mutation', 'expression changes:expression', 'expression changes:expression')) -- CauseOf -- ('leads to', 'Reg', '(81, 89)', ('leads to', 'Reg', '-', '-')) -- ThemeOf -- ('mitochondrial dysfunction', 'CPA', '(90, 115)', ('mitochondrial dysfunction', 'CPA', 'HP:0003287', 'Abnormality of mitochondrial metabolism'))
long_tail_signals.EventDedupKey: term | apoe | expression changes:expression | oxidative stress hypothesis,mitochondrial autophagy hypothesis
long_tail_signals.Sentence: APOE4 expression triggers mitochondrial oxidative stress, reduces ATP synthesis, leads to mitochondrial dysfunction, and subsequently disrupts synaptic transmission and leads to the emergence of cognitive impairment.

neuroinflammation and microglia axis.Sentence: Moreover, recent reports have evidenced that PTN is implicated in the regulation of peripheral metainflammation, metabolic homeostasis, thermogenesis, as well as insulin sensitivity in the peripheral tissues Furthermore, Ptn deletion protects against neuroinflammation, mitochondrial dysfunction, and aberrant protein aggregation in a high fat diet (HFD) induced obesity model.
